symptoms.symptom_name: Dull, constant, heavy right-upper-quadrant ache (capsular stretch)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Capsular stretch from hepatomegaly produces a dull constant ache rather than colic.
Cholecystitis pain is typically sharp/colicky and post-prandial rather than dull and constant.
Fatigue is common in metabolic liver disease and poorly-controlled diabetes but is non-specific.
Absence of fever/rigors makes cholangitis and cholecystitis less likely but does not exclude them.
Vomiting is common with biliary colic/cholecystitis; its absence is mildly reassuring.

symptoms.symptom_name: No jaundice (skin/eyes not yellow, stools/urine normal)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of jaundice argues against biliary obstruction/cholangitis and against decompensation, but self-report is unreliable (patient-observed only).
No GI bleeding reported — no current evidence of portal-hypertensive bleeding.
Central adiposity is a core driver of metabolic steatosis.
Insulin resistance is central to MASLD pathogenesis and predicts fibrosis progression.
Atherogenic dyslipidaemia is part of the metabolic-syndrome cluster underlying steatosis.
Negligible alcohol makes alcohol-related steatosis unlikely and supports a metabolic (non-alcoholic) aetiology.

symptoms.symptom_name: Acanthosis nigricans (dark velvety skin, posterior neck/axillae)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A visible marker of severe insulin resistance; patient- and video-observable, so admissible in telehealth (unverified).

symptoms.symptom_name: Smooth, firm, non-tender hepatomegaly (4 cm below costal margin)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Smooth firm enlargement fits diffuse steatosis; sealed clinician finding — the AI infers likely hepatomegaly but cannot palpate.
A smooth non-tender edge argues against a hard craggy malignant liver, but imaging is still required.
A definitively negative Murphy's sign is strong evidence against acute cholecystitis — but requires palpation the AI cannot do.

symptoms.symptom_name: No ascites, spider naevi or palmar erythema

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of stigmata of chronic liver disease argues against advanced decompensated cirrhosis on exam.

investigation_details.expected_result: Abnormal (pattern consistent with hepatic steatosis); exact values are sealed and never surfaced as raw numbers to AI context
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Missing progression to steatohepatitis/fibrosis or an alternative hepatic cause.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Abnormal LFTs are non-specific but consistent; require a full liver screen to exclude other causes.

investigation_details.expected_result: Diffusely bright (echogenic) liver consistent with steatosis; assesses size and excludes a focal mass
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Steatosis unconfirmed and a focal hepatic lesion not excluded.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A bright liver strongly supports steatosis but does not stage fibrosis; not available in real-time telehealth.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Central obesity + poorly-controlled T2DM + dyslipidaemia + acanthosis nigricans together make metabolic steatosis the leading explanation for the RUQ ache and abnormal LFTs.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Confirms hepatic steatosis, assesses liver size, and excludes a focal mass — the non-urgent first-line image.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Fasting comprehensive metabolic panel + repeat LFTs
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Characterise the LFT abnormality, glycaemic and lipid control.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: FIB-4 fibrosis score (from age, AST, ALT, platelets)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: First-line non-invasive stratification of advanced-fibrosis risk in MASLD.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Viral hepatitis and chronic liver disease screen
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Standard workup of abnormal LFTs before attributing solely to steatosis.
Palpation (hepatomegaly character, tenderness, Murphy's sign, peritonism, ascites) that the AI cannot perform — needed to exclude the acute biliary mimic and guide safe de-escalation.
